answers: Linux is a free, open-source operating system. Used in servers (90%+ of the internet runs on Linux), development, and cybersecurity. Every developer should know basics.
Essential Linux commands: ls, cd, pwd, mkdir, rm, cp, mv, cat, grep, chmod, sudo, apt. These 12 commands handle most daily tasks.
Best Linux distros for beginners: Ubuntu (most popular), Linux Mint (Windows-like), Pop!_OS (developers). Try in a VM first before dual-booting.
Linux tip: learn the terminal - it's more powerful than any GUI. Start with basic navigation (ls, cd, pwd), then file operations, then permissions and scripting.
